title: Iterable and iterator protocols deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Iterable and iterator protocols; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Iterable and iterator protocols: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Iterable and iterator are interchangeable, and calling iter on an exhausted iterator restarts it.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.
Iterable and iterator are interchangeable, and calling iter on an exhausted iterator restarts it.

answer.explanation: An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.

items.title: Iterable and iterator protocols: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Python: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'one-pass state'. An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. An iterable supplies iterators; an iterator carries one-pass state and remains exhausted after StopIteration unless a new iterator is created from a reusable source.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.

items.title: Iterable and iterator protocols: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read a non-negative n. Implement a Countdown iterable that returns a fresh iterator and print n down to 1.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Each call to iter creates independent one-pass state, so the reusable iterable supports two identical traversals.
